description_long: This object is a compact, antique-style blunderbuss shotgun with a flared brass barrel and a dark wooden grip. The barrel is short and widens dramatically at the muzzle, characteristic of early firearms designed for close-range use. The frame and trigger assembly are made of dark metal, with a prominent, rounded trigger guard and a visible hammer mechanism above the grip. The grip is curved and ergonomically shaped, likely made of polished wood, and features a metal ring at the base for attachment. The overall finish is a mix of polished brass and dark, aged metal, giving it a historical, decorative appearance. The object is solid and robust, with no visible modern attachments or modifications.
description: A compact, antique blunderbuss shotgun with a flared brass barrel, dark metal frame, and curved wooden grip. The design is robust and decorative, evoking historical firearms.
description_view_0: Side view showing the flared brass barrel, curved wooden grip, and prominent trigger guard.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide, trumpet-shaped muzzle and the narrow profile of the grip.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same flared barrel and grip, with the hammer and trigger assembly visible.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the rounded end of the grip and the alignment of the barrel.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, flared muzzle with no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard with a smooth, continuous curve and no finger grooves
